user_query:
Read the YAML at input/instructions.yaml and use your local persistent memory to execute the plan exactly as written. Then write these files under output/:

1) output/memory_export.json — an export of the active (non-forgotten) memories after all operations.
2) output/search_results.json — a JSON object mapping each query in the YAML to an array of matching memory keys (only active memories should be considered).
3) output/stats_snapshot.json — a snapshot of your memory stats counters after completing all steps (include at least: total_stores, total_updates, total_deletes, total_retrievals, total_tokens_saved).
4) output/maintenance_report.json — JSON with fields: active_count, archive_count, pruned_count, deduped_count that reflect the final state after maintenance.

Use only relative paths under output/. Do not include any absolute or home-directory paths in the outputs. All outputs must be valid JSON. Make sure the forgotten item is not present in memory_export.json and that search results reflect the updates and the forget operation.
